Package de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.solvertypes

Interface SolverType

All Known Implementing Classes:
SolverTypeImplementation
public interface SolverType
This interface is used for modeling different solvers. It provides methods that encode information about the concrete solver: - name - command for starting the solver - parameters - supported versions

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    checkForSupport()
    Checks for support of the solver type's installed version and returns the result.

  • Method Details

    • createSolver

      SMTSolver createSolver(SMTProblem problem, SolverListener listener, Services services)
      Creates an instance of SMTSolver representing a concrete instance of that solver.
      Parameters:
      problem - the SMT problem that is sent to the resulting SMT solver process
      listener - the listener for the solver process
      services - services object to be used by the solver process, if needed (see for example SMTObjTranslator)
      Returns:
      a concrete solver process of the type at hand

    • getName

      String getName()
      Returns:
      the name of the solver.

    • isInstalled

      boolean isInstalled(boolean recheck)
      Checks whether the solver is installed. If recheck is set to true the method should check for the path variable of the system and for the absolute path, otherwise it can return the result of the previous call.
      Parameters:
      recheck - if this is true, the installation check will be done again instead of returning the result from the previous call of this method
      Returns:
      whether the solver is installed (i.e. the command is an existing file)

    • getInfo

      String getInfo()
      Some specific information about the solver which can be presented. null means no information.
      Returns:
      the solver-type-specific information, null if there is none

    • getSolverParameters

      String getSolverParameters()
      Unless setSolverParameters(String) is called, these should be the same as getDefaultSolverParameters().
      Returns:
      the currently used parameters for the solver.

    • setSolverParameters

      void setSolverParameters(String s)
      Set the solver parameters to be used.
      Parameters:
      s - the new solver parameters

    • getDefaultSolverParameters

      String getDefaultSolverParameters()
      Returns:
      the default parameters which should be used for starting a solver

    • getSolverCommand

      String getSolverCommand()
      The currently used command for starting the solver. For example "z3" if it is registered in the PATH variable, otherwise "ABSOLUTE_PATH/z3". Unless setSolverCommand(String) has been called, this should be the same as getDefaultSolverCommand().
      Returns:
      the currently used solver command for starting solver processes

    • setSolverCommand

      void setSolverCommand(String s)
      Set the solver command to be used for starting processes with the type at hand.
      Parameters:
      s - the new solver command

    • getDefaultSolverCommand

      String getDefaultSolverCommand()
      Returns:
      the default solver command for processes of the type at hand

    • getSolverTimeout

      long getSolverTimeout()
      Unless setSolverTimeout(long) has been called, this should be the same as getDefaultSolverTimeout().
      Returns:
      the currently set solver timeout, e.g. 2000L milliseconds

    • setSolverTimeout

      void setSolverTimeout(long timeout)
      Set the timeout for processes with this solver type.
      Parameters:
      timeout - the new solver timeout

    • getDefaultSolverTimeout

      long getDefaultSolverTimeout()
      Returns:
      the default timeout for processes with this solver type.

    • createTranslator

      SMTTranslator createTranslator()
      Returns:
      the translator to be used. So far each solver supports only one format.

    • getDelimiters

      String[] getDelimiters()
      Message delimiters used by the solver to separate its messages sent to KeY. For example, those could be "\n".
      Returns:
      the delimiters of the messages that are sent from the solver to KeY.

    • modifyProblem

      String modifyProblem(String problem)
      Directly before the problem description is sent to the solver one can modify the problem string by using this method.
      Parameters:
      problem - the SMT problem to modify
      Returns:
      the modified SMT problem String

    • getVersionParameter

      String getVersionParameter()
      Returns:
      the parameter that can be used to gain the version of the solver when executing it.

    • getMinimumSupportedVersion

      String getMinimumSupportedVersion()
      Returns:
      the minimum supported version of the solver.

    • getInstalledVersion

      String getInstalledVersion()
      Returns:
      the current version that is installed, if it has already been checked, otherwise null

    • getRawVersion

      @Nullable String getRawVersion()
      Retrieve the version string without check for support. Returns null if the solver is not installed.
      Returns:
      the installed version of the type at hand, if it is installed - null otherwise

    • isSupportedVersion

      boolean isSupportedVersion()
      Returns:
      whether the currently installed version is supported.

    • checkForSupport

      boolean checkForSupport()
      Checks for support of the solver type's installed version and returns the result.
      Returns:
      true iff the solver type is installed and its version is supported

    • supportHasBeenChecked

      boolean supportHasBeenChecked()
      Returns:
      true iff the support has been checked for the currently installed solver.

    • getSocket

      @Nonnull AbstractSolverSocket getSocket(ModelExtractor query)
      Creates a new solver socket that can handle the communication for the given solver type.
      Parameters:
      query - the ModelExtractor that can be used to extract a counterexample (for non-CE solvers this can be null)
      Returns:
      the newly created socket
